title: Tool Choice, Evidence, Actions, and Approval Boundaries · Prompt Lens: Tool Result Grounding
summary: Design a tool-using investigation that reads evidence before changing state. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt tool result grounding increment in a safe tool-use decision protocol. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Base the next decision on actual tool results and distinguish inference from observation.
Sequence read-only discovery before mutations unless the user explicitly requests an immediate safe action.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Tool Result Grounding
steps.instruction:
Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt tools actions. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

Use a changed theory case derived from this module: prompt tools actions. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. Before reading, predict which action preserves the Tool Result Grounding contract.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for base the next decision on actual tool results and distinguish inference from observation.

content.text: Base the next decision on actual tool results and distinguish inference from observation. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Check preconditions, perform the operation in an inspectable order, then verify the result and a boundary case.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: prompt tools actions. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Tool Result Grounding, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a safe tool-use decision protocol.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A successful tool call proves the intended outcome occurred. This fails the Tool Result Grounding contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Tool Result Grounding evidence

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Tool Result Grounding into observable performance evidence.

Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]

steps.title: Defend the Tool Result Grounding model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Tool Result Grounding applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Base the next decision on actual tool results and distinguish inference from observation.
